title: Gap Fill: The History of Cinema

tasks.instruction: Przeczytaj ponizszy tekst, z ktorego usunieto szesc zdan. Dobierz zdania oznaczone literami (A-H) tak, aby tekst byl spojny i logiczny. Dwa zdania zostaly podane dodatkowo i nie pasuja do zadnego fragmentu.
tasks.text:
THE HISTORY OF CINEMA

The story of cinema begins in the final years of the nineteenth century, when inventors across Europe and America competed to capture and reproduce moving images. _________ (1.1) Their portable device, called the Cinematographe, could record, develop, and project film, and it gave the world its first true cinema experience.

The first public screening took place in a Paris basement on 28 December 1895, before an audience of just thirty-three people. The short films showed everyday scenes: workers leaving a factory, a train arriving at a station. _________ (1.2) According to legend, some audience members fled in panic when the on-screen train appeared to rush toward them.

In the early decades, films were silent, accompanied by live music played in the theatre. Directors quickly developed visual storytelling techniques, including close-ups, cross-cutting between scenes, and the use of lighting to create mood. _________ (1.3) By the 1920s, films had evolved from simple novelties into a sophisticated art form capable of conveying complex narratives and powerful emotions.

The introduction of synchronised sound in 1927, with the release of The Jazz Singer, triggered a revolution that reshaped the entire industry. _________ (1.4) Studios scrambled to convert their equipment and retrain their staff, and within just a few years, silent cinema had virtually disappeared.

Colour film became widespread in the 1950s, precisely when television began to compete for audiences. Cinema attendance declined sharply as families chose to stay home and watch their new television sets. _________ (1.5) Widescreen formats, stereophonic sound, and epic historical productions offered an experience that a small black-and-white television screen could not replicate.

Today, digital technology has transformed every aspect of filmmaking, from shooting and editing to distribution and exhibition. _________ (1.6) Yet for all these technological revolutions, the fundamental appeal of cinema remains what it was in that Paris basement in 1895: the magic of sitting in a darkened room and being transported to another world.

options.text: Among the most successful were the French brothers Auguste and Louis Lumiere, who are widely regarded as the founders of commercial cinema.
Yet what seems unremarkable today was utterly astonishing to an audience that had never seen photographic images move.
These innovations transformed what had been a fairground curiosity into a medium with genuine artistic potential.
Audiences were thrilled to hear actors speak, but many silent-film stars whose voices did not match their screen image saw their careers collapse overnight.
The film industry responded by offering audiences something television could not: scale, spectacle, and technical innovation.
Films can now be shot on lightweight digital cameras, enhanced with computer-generated imagery, and streamed directly to viewers' homes on the day of release.
Hollywood's studio system, in which a handful of major companies controlled production, distribution, and exhibition, dominated the industry from the 1920s to the 1960s.
National cinema traditions in countries such as India, Japan, and France have produced distinctive styles that have influenced filmmakers worldwide.
